title: SoD violations via break-glass usage — emergency role abuse
exclusions: Pairs with UC-22.40.2 for post-use approval.
evidence: Real-time 'breakglass_sod_violation' detects SoD violations during break-glass usage.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Break-glass that crosses SoD boundaries must be compensated; this UC generates the signal at session close.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOX ITGC ITGC.AccessMgmt.SOD (Segregation of duties) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.48.2: SoD violations via break-glass usage — emergency role abuse.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.48.2' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When a break-glass session performs actions that cross an SoD boundary (per sod_matrix.csv) without a pre-approved emergency-exception ticket, the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Break-glass sessions that stay within the permitted action set do NOT fire.

spl:
index=pam breakglass=true earliest=-24h
| stats values(action_category) as actions by session_id account
| mvexpand actions
| lookup sod_matrix.csv action_a=actions OUTPUT toxic_against
| mvexpand toxic_against
| join session_id toxic_against [search index=pam breakglass=true earliest=-24h | eval hit=action_category]
| join session_id [search index=grc sourcetype=emergency:exception | fields session_id exception_id]
| where isnull(exception_id)
| table session_id account actions toxic_against
description: Detects break-glass sessions that executed actions which would normally violate segregation of duties. Unless there is an emergency-exception ticket, the session breaches SoD even if the break-glass approval is otherwise valid.
value: Break-glass is often abused to step around SoD; this UC catches that pattern without waiting for audit.
implementation: (1) Tag PAM session actions into SoD categories; (2) maintain sod_matrix.csv; (3) require emergency exceptions to log to GRC; (4) run hourly. Indexes required: `index=grc`, `index=pam`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=emergency:exception`.
visualization: Table of sessions with SoD crossings, single-value 'SoD-compliant emergency sessions'. Key fields: `account`, `actions`, `hit`, `session_id`, `toxic_against`.
knownFalsePositives: SoD violations during scheduled role changes, contractor offboarding gaps, or controlled segregation review windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag PAM session actions into SoD categories; (2) maintain sod_matrix.csv; (3) require emergency exceptions to log to GRC; (4) run hourly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=pam breakglass=true earliest=-24h
| stats values(action_category) as actions by session_id account
| mvexpand actions
| lookup sod_matrix.csv action_a=actions OUTPUT toxic_against
| mvexpand toxic_against
| join session_id toxic_against [search index=pam breakglass=true earliest=-24h | eval hit=action_category]
| join session_id [search index=grc sourcetype=emergency:exception | fields session_id exception_id]
| where isnull(exception_id)
| table session_id account actions toxic_against
```

Understanding this SPL

**SoD violations via break-glass usage — emergency role abuse** — Break-glass is often abused to step around SoD; this UC catches that pattern without waiting for audit.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: pam.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=pam, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **session_id account**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Expands multivalue fields with `mvexpand` — use `limit=` to cap row explosion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Expands multivalue fields with `mvexpand` — use `limit=` to cap row explosion.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(exception_id)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: account, actions, hit, session_id, toxic_against.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the identity, HR, or SoD tool of record and compare with the GRC system so segregation rules match live roles.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of sessions with SoD crossings, single-value 'SoD-compliant emergency sessions'.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see when the same person could do two jobs that should be split, so errors and fraud are harder.
